physicalDetails.detail: Stocky dwarf commander cut from his own siege-worn camp: beard rimed with frost, iron rings clicking when he turns his head, and a hammer riding his back with the ease of long practice.

Chapter 22 leaves Muradin as a body on the edge between witness and loss: blood thawed and frozen again in his beard, hauled on a hide stretcher through Baelgun’s camp and alive only in the hard practical sense.

voiceMarkers.detail: Blunt, practical, and warm in the dwarf way. He moves easily between threat, briefing, and rough humor, and cuts magical jargon short with demands like Speak dwarf.
His one live verbal beat in chapter22 keeps the chapter-21 pattern intact — Speak dwarf — blunt enough to force plain sense out of magical horror even while he is already fading.

beliefs.belief: Arthas was already far enough gone before the cave that ordinary warning or command logic would not turn him back.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter21 by both Muradin’s briefing and Arthas’s state in the cavern.

beliefs.belief: Frostmourne is a cursed blade no sensible man should want, and anyone trying to stop Arthas needs the local route truth immediately rather than later.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Survive Baelgun’s treatment long enough for the cave warning to matter later.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Remain the wounded proof that Arthas and Frostmourne can still be borne witness against even after the cave defeat.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Off-page under treatment, alive enough to keep but too deeply wounded to participate in the next movement south.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Lets Sylvanas’s party into the camp, spends his last useful intelligence on them immediately, and chooses to guide them himself instead of hiding behind the wall he built.
Faces Arthas first in the cave and physically commits to the confrontation even after the evidence outside proves they are too late.
Forces one last piece of useful translation into the chapter before collapsing fully, demanding plain speech about the sword from Jaina.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Turns a siege-worn field camp into a functioning last foothold through fortification, discipline, and hard local knowledge.
Compresses route, curse lore, and personality read into a briefing that gives the pursuers exactly the information they need and nothing decorative.
Fights Arthas in close quarters long enough to prove the scale of the mismatch before the shard strike takes him down.
Even at collapse, Muradin can still force a magical report into field-usable language.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Jaina Proudmoore
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Uses Muradin with old familiarity rather than rank.

howHeAddressesOthers.pattern: Calls her Lady before the room settles into practical work, mixing courtesy with old familiarity.
Drops to blunt personal challenge once the blade is on the prince’s hand.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Baelgun Flamebeard and the formation
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Handled as Muradin in the immediate urgency of casualty transfer, proof of his standing and familiarity inside the expedition.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Comes out of the shelter already striding hard, reads the newcomers in one sweep, and plants both hands on the table when Jaina finally gives him language for what Frostmourne is.
His head rolls once toward Jaina on the stretcher and then the rest of the chapter treats him as weight, blood, and the uncertain work of keeping him alive.

knowledge.detail: Knows Arthas passed the camp roughly two days earlier, already running on obsession rather than command sense.
Knows the cave route, the warning inscription on Frostmourne, and the local terrain well enough to guide a lighter force there quickly.
Leaves the chapter having seen Jaina identify Frostmourne as kin to the plague, though he is unconscious before he can act on what that means next.
Chapter 22 confirms Muradin got out of the cave alive enough to hand over to Baelgun’s healers, preserving the story’s last living witness to Arthas before and after Frostmourne.

arc.currentPosition: Chapter 22 moves Muradin off the active board without removing him from continuity. He becomes the wounded witness kept alive at camp while the others race south with what he helped make legible.

goalsHistory.active: Survive long enough to be handed off at camp after the cave disaster.
goalsHistory.underlying: Stop Arthas if possible and, failing that, make sure the people chasing him leave the cave with the truth about Frostmourne.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Unconscious and critically wounded by extraction’s end, but alive enough that the others treat him as a recoverable casualty rather than a body.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Muradin enters as the last living witness to Arthas before the blade and leaves as the wounded proof that the warning came too late. His chapter function is to name the partnership from outside and survive long enough to carry the cave’s truth back to camp.
